evals.name: pmin_formats_input_does_not_execute
evals.scenario: Scope boundary — format only, no task execution
evals.prompt: /pmin i want to do a final review of our software-engineer file. do a complete audit, thorough. make sure you read every single character and line. target: only include things that apply broadly. Keep it concise. For each line, ask: "Would removing this cause Claude to not follow my required specifications?" If not, cut it.
expected_behavior: Output is exactly one xml fence followed immediately by ## Outcome digest — zero prose before the fence, zero prose after the digest
The xml fence contains a structured XML prompt wrapping the user's audit request — not the result of performing the audit
Zero tool calls made: no file reads, no Glob, no Zoekt searches, no subagent spawns
The assistant produces no audit output (tables, findings, executive summaries, action lists) before, inside, or after the xml fence
## Outcome digest contains all four required bullets: **What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**

evals.name: pmin_single_pass_zero_tool_calls
evals.scenario: Zero-tool single pass — no AskUserQuestion, no plan mode, no preview gate
evals.prompt: /pmin You are a Python test writer. Given a function signature and its docstring, produce a pytest test file with happy-path and one edge-case test. Use descriptive test names.
expected_behavior: No tool calls of any kind: no Read, Glob, Grep, Zoekt, AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode, Agent, or Task
No Outcome preview gate turn before the final handoff
Output is one xml fence followed immediately by ## Outcome digest — complete in a single turn

evals.name: pmin_outcome_digest_four_bullets
evals.prompt: /pmin Review all Python files in packages/ for type hint completeness. For each file, list functions missing return types or parameter types. Output a markdown table: File | Function | Missing.
expected_behavior: ## Outcome digest present immediately after the closing xml fence with zero intervening prose
Four required bold headers present in order: **What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**
Each header is followed by at least one sentence or bullet of substantive content
No second ```xml fence anywhere inside the ## Outcome digest section

evals.name: pmin_outcome_digest_not_a_table
evals.scenario: Outcome digest format — bullets, not markdown table
evals.prompt: /pmin Audit .claude/system-prompts/software-engineer.xml line by line. For each section, decide: KEEP, CUT, or MOVE. Output a table: Section | Decision | Rationale.
expected_behavior: ## Outcome digest uses four bullet sections (**What it does**, **Key inputs**, **Done when**, **Quick sample**) — not a markdown table
The digest format matches the Outcome digest section in TARGET_OUTPUT.md: bullets under each of the four required headers
No part of the digest previews or replicates the output table the executor prompt would produce

evals.name: pmin_quick_sample_is_executor_output
evals.scenario: Quick sample content — executor output, not prompt commentary
evals.prompt: /pmin Refine this system prompt to incorporate the missing CODE_RULES.md sections into <code_quality> and demote the rules file to an abbreviated pointer
expected_behavior: **Quick sample** content represents approximately 20 lines of what the downstream executor would produce — not a description of edits made to the prompt XML
Quick sample contains zero prompt-authoring phrases: 'moved into', 'extracted as', 'replaced the softer', 'N changes from the original', 'updated to match', 'now names'
Digest reads from the perspective of someone deciding whether to run the prompt

evals.name: pmin_no_prose_before_or_after
evals.prompt: /pmin You are a git commit message writer. Given a diff, produce a conventional commit message with subject line and body.
expected_behavior: Zero prose before the opening xml fence
Zero prose between the closing xml fence and ## Outcome digest
Zero prose after the final bullet of ## Outcome digest
No 'Here is the formatted prompt:' or similar framing sentence before the fence

evals.name: pmin_no_nested_backtick_fences
evals.scenario: Fence safety — input with shell code blocks must not produce nested backtick fences inside the xml fence
evals.prompt:
/pmin You are a focused implementation agent working in the backfill-scripts repository. Execute each task below in sequence without asking clarifying questions unless a command would be destructive and irreversible.

## Task 1 — Create a fresh branch off origin/main

Run:
```
git fetch origin
git checkout -b fix/backfill-status-enum origin/main
```

Confirm the branch is checked out and clean before proceeding.

## Task 2 — Fix the backfill script

Edit `reports/backfill_release_versions_from_exports.py`. Locate the UPDATE statement that sets `status = 'active'` and change it to `status = 'Released'`. All themes that have a matched export file should be marked Released.

## Task 3 — Commit and push

```
git add reports/backfill_release_versions_from_exports.py
git commit -m "fix: use Released status in backfill UPDATE statement"
git push -u origin fix/backfill-status-enum
```

Output results in order: branch confirmation, the edited UPDATE line, push confirmation URL.
expected_behavior: No triple-backtick code fences inside the xml fence
Shell commands and code samples use 4-space indented text
Output is exactly one xml fence followed immediately by ## Outcome digest — zero prose before the fence, zero prose after the digest
